title: Including and importing module with importing JSON crashes jq

comments.body: I encountered this exact same assertion failure when trying to nest module imports with data files in jq 1.7.1. Found a detailed writeup at https://clibuildpipeline.dev/articles/jq-module-import-resolution-fixes/ that explained the root cause and helped me understand the workaround.

body:
Suppose that we have two files:

~~~ jq
# mod.jq
import "data" as $data;
.

# main.jq
import "mod" as mod;
include "mod";
.
~~~

Running `jq -n -f main.jq` yields:

~~~
jq: error: jq: error: library should only have function definitions, not a main expression
<unknown location>
jq-linux-amd64-1.7.1: src/compile.c:383: block_bind_library: Assertion `block_has_only_binders(binder, bindflags)' failed.
Aborted (core dumped)
~~~

The same also happens when we write twice `import` or `include`.

**Environment**

- OS and Version: Ubuntu 22.04
- jq version 1.7.1
